content.full_text:
Fern Polrrtcel PRACTICEs CouvtlsSIoN
428 J Street o Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 r Fax (916) 322-0886
December 7,2007
Charles Ciali
Public Arts Commissioner
234 N. Helena Circle
Palm Springs, CA 92262
RE: Your Request for Opinion
Our File No. W-07-187
Dear Mr. Ciali:
This letter addresses your complaint sent to the Commission's Enforcement
Division dated November 25,2007. The Legal Division received a copy of the request
since in it you asked for a written opinion on the law.
After review of your letter, we decline to provide written advice. Your questions
involve analysis of facts, which have already occurred, such that issuing written advice at
this time would relate to past conduct. Moreover, it does not appear that Mr. Stern has
authorized you to request advice on his behalf. Unless Mr. Stern has authorized you as
his representative, and the request involves prospective conduct the Fair Political
Practices Commission cannot address your questions or provide an analysis. (Regulation
18329(bX8XA) and (cX4)(A), copy enclosed.)
Should you have any additional questions on this matter, please contact the
Commission at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
t
General Counsel
By: ', John W. Wallace
Assistant General Counsel
...t' Legal Division

Unless Mr. Stern has authorized you as his representative, and the request involves prospective conduct the Fair Political Practices Commission cannot address your questions or provide an analysis.
sections.question_synthetic: The requester asked for written FPPC advice regarding analysis of past conduct involving Mr. Stern, but it is unclear whether Mr. Stern authorized the requester to request advice on his behalf.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide written advice because the questions involve analysis of past conduct rather than prospective conduct, and Mr. Stern does not appear to have authorized the requester to seek advice on his behalf.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination letter (file number W-07-187). No traditional QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections with headers were present; both were extracted from body text. The letter references enclosed regulations 18329(b)(8)(A) and (c)(4)(A).

QUESTION: The requester asked for written FPPC advice regarding analysis of past conduct involving Mr. Stern, but it is unclear whether Mr. Stern authorized the requester to request advice on his behalf.

CONCLUSION: After review of your letter, we decline to provide written advice. Your questions involve analysis of facts, which have already occurred, such that issuing written advice at this time would relate to past conduct. Moreover, it does not appear that Mr. Stern has authorized you to request advice on his behalf. Unless Mr. Stern has authorized you as his representative, and the request involves prospective conduct the Fair Political Practices Commission cannot address your questions or provide an analysis.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declined a request for written advice from Charles Ciali, a Public Arts Commissioner, regarding conduct analysis because the matters involved past conduct rather than prospective conduct and lacked evidence of authorization from the relevant party, Mr. Stern.
